title: IISc Administrative Assistant Guess Paper 1
subtitle: Realistic prediction based on 2023 examiner patterns
instructions: Total 80 questions to be attempted in 90 minutes.
Negative marking applies: 0.33333 marks (one-third) are deducted for every incorrect answer.
Figure-based placeholder questions are unscored and carry 0 marks either way.

questions.text: A's salary is 25% more than B's salary. By what percentage is B's salary less than A's salary?
questions.explanation: B is less by 25/125 = 20%.

questions.text: A shopkeeper marks his goods 40% above cost price and allows a discount of 15%. His profit percentage is:
questions.explanation: SP = 1.40 x 0.85 = 1.19 of CP, so profit is 19%.

questions.text: A can finish a work in 12 days and B in 18 days. A, B and C together finish it in 6 days. In how many days can C alone finish the work?
A 240 m long train crosses a 360 m long platform in 30 seconds. The speed of the train is:
A boat's speed in still water is 9 km/h and the stream flows at 3 km/h. The total time taken to go 24 km downstream and return is:
A 40-litre mixture contains milk and water in the ratio 7:3. How much water must be added to make the ratio 7:5?
questions.explanation: Milk 28 L needs water 20 L; add 20 - 12 = 8 L.

questions.text: Enter the simple interest (in rupees) on Rs 8000 at 7.5% per annum for 4 years.
A invests Rs 5000 for 12 months and B invests Rs 6000 for 8 months in a business. Out of a total profit of Rs 9900, B's share is:
The largest number which divides 245 and 1029 leaving remainder 5 in each case is:
Simplify: (3/4 of 2/5) divided by 3/10, plus 1/2.
A father is three times as old as his son. After 12 years he will be twice as old as his son. The father's present age is:
questions.explanation: 3S + 12 = 2(S + 12) gives S = 12, father 36.

questions.text: Pipe A fills a tank in 10 hours, pipe B in 15 hours, and pipe C empties it in 30 hours. If all three are opened together, the tank fills in:
Ram spends 80% of his income. His income rises by 20% and his expenditure rises by 10%. His savings increase by:
questions.explanation: Savings go from 20 to 120 - 88 = 32, a 60% rise.

questions.text: A right circular cone has base radius 7 cm and height 24 cm. Its curved surface area is: (use pi = 22/7)
questions.explanation: Slant height 25; CSA = (22/7) x 7 x 25 = 550.

questions.text: The graphs of 2x + 3y = 12 and x - y = 1 intersect at the point:
questions.explanation: Solving gives x = 3, y = 2.

questions.text: A chord of length 48 cm is at a distance of 7 cm from the centre of a circle. The radius of the circle is:

Sentence 1: The invention of paper transformed civilisation. Arrange P, Q, R, S to follow it. P: These materials were bulky and fragile. Q: Before paper, records were kept on clay tablets and palm leaves. R: As a result, knowledge spread faster than ever before. S: Paper, by contrast, was cheap, light and durable.
questions.explanation: Old materials, their flaw, paper's contrast, then the result.

questions.text: Sentence 1: Water scarcity is emerging as a global crisis. Arrange P, Q, R, S. P: Cities are already rationing supply in summer months. Q: Groundwater tables are falling at an alarming rate. R: Unless usage changes, shortages will become permanent. S: Erratic rainfall has made the situation worse.
questions.explanation: Causes (groundwater, rainfall), present effect, future warning.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To bite the bullet

options.text: To face a painful situation bravely

questions.explanation: It means enduring something unpleasant with courage.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: A dime a dozen
questions.explanation: It describes something abundant and of little value.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To throw down the gauntlet
questions.explanation: It means to challenge someone openly.

questions.text: One who can use both hands equally well:
questions.explanation: Ambidextrous means skilled with both hands.

questions.text: Government by a few:
questions.explanation: Oligarchy is rule by a small group.

questions.text: Select the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: Double c and double r: occurrence.

questions.text: Select the incorrectly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is definitely.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: He has been working here SINCE five years.
questions.explanation: Use 'for' with a period of time.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: HARDLY HE HAD reached the station when the train left.
questions.explanation: Negative adverb at the start requires inversion.

questions.text: Select the synonym of CANDID:
questions.explanation: Candid means open and honest.

questions.text: Select the antonym of FRUGAL:
questions.explanation: Frugal (sparing) is opposite to extravagant.

questions.passage: Honeybees are among the most efficient pollinators on the planet. As they move from flower to flower collecting nectar, pollen sticks to their bodies and fertilises the plants they visit. Nearly a third of the food crops humans consume depend on such pollination. Yet bee populations are declining worldwide due to pesticide use, habitat loss and climate change. Scientists warn that this silent collapse could threaten global food security. Several countries have now restricted certain pesticides and encouraged farmers to plant wildflowers along field margins to give bees safe foraging grounds.
questions.text: According to the passage, bee populations are declining because of:

options.text: Pesticides, habitat loss and climate change

questions.explanation: The passage lists these three causes directly.

questions.passage: Honeybees are among the most efficient pollinators on the planet. As they move from flower to flower collecting nectar, pollen sticks to their bodies and fertilises the plants they visit. Nearly a third of the food crops humans consume depend on such pollination. Yet bee populations are declining worldwide due to pesticide use, habitat loss and climate change. Scientists warn that this silent collapse could threaten global food security. Several countries have now restricted certain pesticides and encouraged farmers to plant wildflowers along field margins to give bees safe foraging grounds.
questions.text: The most appropriate title for the passage is:

options.text: Bees and the Future of Food

questions.explanation: The passage links bee decline to food security.

questions.passage: Honeybees are among the most efficient pollinators on the planet. As they move from flower to flower collecting nectar, pollen sticks to their bodies and fertilises the plants they visit. Nearly a third of the food crops humans consume depend on such pollination. Yet bee populations are declining worldwide due to pesticide use, habitat loss and climate change. Scientists warn that this silent collapse could threaten global food security. Several countries have now restricted certain pesticides and encouraged farmers to plant wildflowers along field margins to give bees safe foraging grounds.
questions.text: In the passage, the word 'foraging' most nearly means:
questions.explanation: Foraging means looking for food.

questions.text: Find the next term: 7, 12, 22, 37, 57, ?
questions.explanation: Differences 5, 10, 15, 20, then 25.

questions.text: Find the next term: 3, 4, 8, 17, 33, ?
questions.explanation: Differences are squares: 1, 4, 9, 16, then 25.

questions.text: Find the next term: 5, 6, 14, 45, 184, ?
questions.explanation: Pattern x1+1, x2+2, x3+3, x4+4, then 184 x 5 + 5.

questions.text: Find the next term: BD, GI, LN, QS, ?
questions.explanation: Each pair starts 5 letters ahead: B, G, L, Q, V.

questions.text: If the digits of 7346285 are arranged in ascending order, how many digits remain in the same position?
questions.explanation: 2345678 vs 7346285: the 3 and 4 stay in place.

questions.text: In a code, PLANT is written as QNDRY. How is MOUSE written in that code?
questions.explanation: Letters shift by +1, +2, +3, +4, +5.

questions.text: If CAT = 24 and DOG = 26, then COW = ?
questions.explanation: Sum of letter positions: 3 + 15 + 23 = 41.

questions.text: In a code language, 'sky is blue' = 'pa ta ra', 'sea is deep' = 'ma ta la' and 'sky looks clear' = 'pa jo so'. What is the code for 'is'?
questions.explanation: 'ta' is common to the first two sentences.

questions.text: Five friends P, Q, R, S, T sit in a row facing north. R is to the immediate right of Q. S is at the extreme right. P sits between T and Q. Who sits in the middle?
questions.explanation: Order is T, P, Q, R, S; Q is in the middle.

questions.text: Six friends A, B, C, D, E, F sit around a circular table. B is between A and C, D is between C and E, and F is between E and A. Who sits opposite C?
questions.explanation: Circle order A, B, C, D, E, F puts F opposite C.

questions.text: In a class of 40 students, Ravi ranks 12th from the top. His rank from the bottom is:
If the day before yesterday was Saturday, what day will it be the day after tomorrow?
questions.explanation: Today is Monday, so day after tomorrow is Wednesday.

questions.text: Pointing to a photograph, Ram said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the girl related to Ram?
questions.explanation: Grandfather's only son is Ram's father; his daughter is Ram's sister.

questions.text: A + B means A is the father of B, A - B means A is the brother of B, A x B means A is the sister of B. Which expression shows that P is the uncle of Q?
questions.explanation: P is brother of R and R is father of Q, so P is uncle.

questions.text: A man walks 5 km north, turns right and walks 3 km, then turns right again and walks 5 km. How far and in which direction is he from the start?
questions.explanation: The northward legs cancel, leaving 3 km east.

questions.text: The approximate value of 24.98 x 3.99 + 74.99 / 5.01 is:
If the signs 'x' and '/' are interchanged, and '+' and '-' are interchanged, the value of 16 x 4 / 2 + 6 - 3 is:
ACE : BDF :: PRT : ?
questions.explanation: Each letter moves one step forward.

questions.text: Find the odd one out: BDG, CEH, DFI, EGK
questions.explanation: The pattern is +2, +3; EGK should be EGJ.

questions.text: Statements: All pens are books. Some books are chairs. Conclusions: I. Some pens are chairs. II. Some chairs are books.
questions.explanation: II is the converse of a given statement; I is not certain.

questions.text: Select the figure that can replace the question mark (?) in the given figure series.

figure.label: Figural Series
figure.description: A sequence of four figures with a rotating shaded segment; choose the fifth figure from four options.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual series question.

questions.text: Select the correct mirror image of the given figure when the mirror is placed to its right.

figure.label: Mirror Image
figure.description: A composite letter-number figure whose mirror image must be chosen from four options.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual mirror-image question.

questions.text: Who won the men's singles title at Wimbledon 2024?
questions.explanation: Alcaraz beat Djokovic in the 2024 final.

questions.text: The Nobel Peace Prize 2024 was awarded to:
questions.explanation: The Japanese atomic-bomb survivors' organisation won.

questions.text: ISRO's SpaDeX mission, launched in orbit in early 2025, successfully demonstrated:
questions.explanation: SpaDeX made India the fourth nation to dock satellites in space.

questions.text: In February 2025, the RBI cut the repo rate for the first time in about five years, bringing it to:
questions.explanation: The rate was reduced from 6.50% to 6.25%.

questions.text: The Servants of India Society (1905) was founded by:
questions.explanation: Gokhale founded it in Pune in 1905.

questions.text: The Dandi March of 1930 began from:
questions.explanation: Gandhi walked from Sabarmati Ashram to Dandi.

questions.text: The Hirakud Dam is built on which river?
questions.explanation: Hirakud in Odisha is on the Mahanadi.

questions.text: The Maha Kumbh Mela 2025 was held at Prayagraj in which state?
questions.explanation: Prayagraj is in Uttar Pradesh.

questions.text: Garba of Gujarat was inscribed on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list in:
questions.explanation: Garba was inscribed in December 2023.

questions.text: The Right to Constitutional Remedies is guaranteed under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 32 allows citizens to approach the Supreme Court.

questions.text: The book 'Why Bharat Matters' is authored by:
questions.explanation: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar wrote it.

questions.text: Naypyidaw is the capital of which of India's neighbouring countries?
questions.explanation: Myanmar shifted its capital from Yangon to Naypyidaw.

questions.text: D. Gukesh, a Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna awardee, became the youngest world champion in 2024 in which game?
questions.explanation: Gukesh won the World Chess Championship in December 2024.

questions.text: The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) was developed by:
questions.explanation: The National Payments Corporation of India built UPI.

questions.text: The Chandrayaan-3 landing site on the Moon has been named:
questions.explanation: The touchdown site was named Shiv Shakti Point.

questions.text: The high-level committee on 'One Nation, One Election' was headed by:
questions.explanation: Former President Ram Nath Kovind chaired the panel.

questions.text: In Excel, A1 = 8, A2 = 3, A3 = 5. What does the formula =A1+A2*A3-A1/2 return?
questions.explanation: 8 + 15 - 4 = 19, following operator precedence.

questions.text: Which Excel function counts only the cells that contain numbers in a range?
questions.explanation: COUNT counts numeric cells; COUNTA counts all non-empty cells.

questions.text: In MS Word, the shortcut Ctrl + E is used to:
questions.explanation: Ctrl + E centres the selected paragraph.

questions.text: Pressing Windows key + L on a Windows PC will:
questions.explanation: Windows + L locks the screen.

questions.text: Which of the following is a non-volatile memory?
questions.explanation: ROM retains data without power.

questions.text: RAM is called a volatile memory because:

options.text: Its contents are lost when power is switched off

questions.explanation: RAM loses its data when power goes off.

questions.text: A disk defragmenter is an example of:
questions.explanation: It is a maintenance utility.

questions.text: Which of the following is system software?
questions.explanation: The OS manages hardware and other software.

questions.text: A compiler is a program that:

options.text: Executes a program line by line
Translates the whole high-level program into machine code at once

questions.explanation: A compiler translates the entire source program in one go.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a computer?
questions.explanation: A computer has no native intelligence; it follows instructions.
